candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit, but it is a company-level total and not specifically identified as COGS/cost of sales related. Adding it to COGS would risk misallocating SG&A-related D&A.
Component of total D&A, but still not labelled as COGS-specific. Also inferior to the summary D&A row if a D&A row were to be chosen.
Component of total D&A and not labelled as COGS-specific; likely overlaps with the summary D&A row.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Raw materials and change in inventories'. The statement shows a separate operating-level 'Depreciation and amortisation expenses' row outside that bucket, but it is not disaggregated into COGS vs SG&A. To avoid double counting or misallocation, no COGS-specific D&A row is added automatically.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit and therefore appears outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Employee benefit expenses' and 'Other operating expenses'). However, it is not specifically labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead D&A, so assigning the full amount to XSGA would risk including production-related D&A.
Component of total D&A, not SG&A-specific, and should not be preferred over the summary row.
Component of total D&A, not SG&A-specific, and may include non-SG&A amortisation. Inferior to the summary row if allocation were clear.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Employee benefit expenses' plus 'Other operating expenses'. A separate total D&A line exists at the same operating level, so some D&A may sit outside the selected XSGA rows, but the excerpt does not indicate what portion belongs to SG&A versus COGS. XRD is empty and there is no explicit R&D expense row above operating profit. Conservative output is no automatic XSGA_DA addition pending manual allocation.
